item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its own pre-release models
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

item.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

item.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Sila's fresh $300 million round will help it make enough of its silicon-carbon anode material to power more than 100,000 EVs.

item.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The acquisition — Einride's first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business.

item.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

item.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

item.title: Tesla spins up robotaxi pilots in Orlando and Tampa ahead of Q2 earnings
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company didn't say how many are in each city and has taken a far more cautious approach to scaling the network than CEO Elon Musk had promised.

item.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

item.title: Instagram now lets you swap out the music in your old posts
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: With the new "Replace Audio" tool, users can update the music on their post at any time while keeping the post's existing likes, comments, shares, and reach intact.

item.title: Threads rolls out parental supervision tools
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: With the new tools, parents and guardians will be able to view their teen's time spent on Threads, set daily time limits, adjust sleep mode, and manage their privacy settings.

item.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

item.title: Light made a flip phone — it’s colorful and it’s cheap
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Light co-founder Kaiwei Tang helped create the Motorola Razr, and he's as surprised as anyone that over 20 years later, the flip phone is relevant again.

item.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: TikTok recently settled its portion of the case with the plaintiff ahead of a jury trial that starts next week in Los Angeles. YouTube has also reached a deal, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant.

item.title: AI music generator Suno breach affects 55M users, per Have I Been Pwned
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: A hacker took names, phone numbers, and physical addresses of millions of customers who used AI music generator Suno.

item.title: What to know about the landmark Warner Bros. Discovery sale
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Learn more about Paramount's planned acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery — a historic Hollywood megadeal valued at $111 billion — as it continues to develop.

item.title: UK government scraps plans for digital ID cards after millions of Brits opposed
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The new U.K. government scrapped a digital ID program that millions of British people opposed, in place of a tax cut to tackle the cost of living.

item.title: The Xteink X4 Pro could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Xteink makes its pocket-sized e-reader even better with a touchscreen and front light.

item.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.
